rows.legacy_behavior: Legacy JAX hashes compiled computation bytes plus devices, compile options, backend metadata, and version material.
rows.rust_behavior: FrankenJAX hashes canonical Jaxpr structure and all Rust-side execution configuration with SHA-256.
rows.strict_behavior: Rejects unknown incompatible feature material before hashing.
rows.hardened_behavior: Hashes unknown feature material into the key so progress remains auditable.

rows.legacy_behavior: Legacy JAX includes compiler flags and codegen-affecting options in the hash.
rows.rust_behavior: Rust compile options are sorted by BTreeMap key order before hashing.
rows.strict_behavior: Sorted options are hashed only when all material is recognized.
rows.hardened_behavior: Sorted options are hashed together with unknown feature material.

rows.legacy_behavior: Legacy JAX includes device assignment and platform identity to prevent cross-device artifact reuse.
rows.rust_behavior: V1 Rust cache keys include backend identity. Current runtime scope is CPU, while non-CPU strings still separate keys.
rows.strict_behavior: Unknown backend semantics must be represented as incompatible feature material by callers.
rows.hardened_behavior: Backend string still separates keys for defensive diagnostics.

rows.legacy_behavior: Legacy JAX compilation caches distinguish transformed function state and abstract input state.
rows.rust_behavior: Rust cache keys preserve exact transform order; grad,vmap and vmap,grad hash differently.
rows.strict_behavior: Transform stack is required key material.
rows.hardened_behavior: Transform stack is required key material.

rows.legacy_behavior: Legacy JAX includes JAX version material in the hash and stores raw hex keys.
rows.rust_behavior: Rust uses the fjx namespace as explicit coarse version material for all cache keys.
rows.strict_behavior: Namespace mismatch is a fail-closed cache miss across versions.
rows.hardened_behavior: Namespace mismatch is a diagnostic cache miss; no stale artifact is accepted.

rows.legacy_behavior: Legacy JAX hashes known metadata and versioned compiler options.
rows.rust_behavior: Rust makes unknown incompatible feature material explicit at the API boundary.
rows.strict_behavior: Strict mode returns CacheKeyError::UnknownIncompatibleFeatures without producing a key.
rows.hardened_behavior: Hardened mode hashes the feature list so distinct unknowns cannot alias clean keys.

rows.legacy_behavior: Legacy JAX cache behavior includes function identity and hook-like wrapping state in memoization layers.
rows.rust_behavior: Rust exposes custom_hook as explicit key material instead of implicit function identity.
rows.strict_behavior: Hook material changes the key only when the caller supplies recognized hook state.
rows.hardened_behavior: Hook material changes the key and can be diagnosed in lifecycle reports.

rows.legacy_behavior: Legacy JAX can read/write compiled executable artifacts through memory, file, and cloud cache layers.
rows.rust_behavior: Rust V1 has cache-manager primitives and deterministic keys, but dispatch still interprets directly instead of compiling XLA executables.
rows.strict_behavior: Compiled-artifact cache misses fall back to recomputation/interpreted execution.
rows.hardened_behavior: Malformed cache state is reported as corrupted or missed without accepting stale bytes.

rows.legacy_behavior: Legacy JAX treats missing or incompatible cached executables as cache misses and recompiles.
rows.rust_behavior: Rust file cache embeds payload digests and surfaces corrupt reads as CacheLookup::Corrupted.
rows.strict_behavior: Corrupt reads are not accepted as hits.
rows.hardened_behavior: Corrupt reads are not accepted as hits; recovery must be audited by callers.

rows.legacy_behavior: Legacy JAX writes cache artifacts atomically and treats write failures as non-fatal cache misses.
rows.rust_behavior: Rust file cache writes serialized artifacts through a temporary file and rename; failed writes do not create readable hits.
rows.strict_behavior: A failed write cannot become a strict cache hit.
rows.hardened_behavior: A failed write cannot become a hardened cache hit.

rows.legacy_behavior: Legacy JAX hashes raw serialized fields so delimiter-like bytes are part of the digest input.
rows.rust_behavior: Rust hashes delimiter-bearing strings as bytes in their field positions; lifecycle tests prove hostile material does not alias clean keys.
rows.strict_behavior: Hostile unknown material is rejected when classified incompatible.
rows.hardened_behavior: Hostile unknown material is hashed and separated from clean key material.

rows.legacy_behavior: Legacy JAX can use Google Cloud Storage as a persistent compilation cache backend.
rows.rust_behavior: FrankenJAX V1 intentionally excludes cloud cache backends; local file and in-memory cache primitives cover current scope.
rows.strict_behavior: Cloud cache URIs must not be silently accepted.
rows.hardened_behavior: Cloud cache URIs must not be silently accepted.
rows.exclusion_reason: GCS compilation cache is outside V1 CPU/runtime scope and would require credentials, network policy, and artifact lifecycle controls.
